sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: Link configuration field.

description.para: Address mapping for the DisplayPort TX core.

briefdescription.para: Disable scrambler and transmit all symbols.
Enable a 0.5% spreading of the clock.

sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: 80-bit custom patterns for link quality test.
DPPSU core registers: Core enables.

briefdescription.para: Enable the basic operations of the DisplayPort TX core or output stuffing symbols if disabled.
Enable transmission of main link video info.

sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: Core ID.
DPPSU core registers: AUX channel interface.

briefdescription.para: Write data for the current AUX command.
Specifies the address of current AUX command.
Clock divider value for generating the internal 1MHz clock.
Indicates an overflow in user FIFO.
The raw signal values for interrupt events.
Reply data received during the AUX reply.
Reply code received from the most recent AUX command.
Number of reply transactions received over AUX.
Total number of data bytes actually received during a transaction.
Reply status of most recent AUX transaction.
Duration of the HPD pulse in microseconds.

sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: Main stream attributes.

briefdescription.para: Total number of clocks in the horizontal framing period.
Total number of lines in the video frame.
Polarity for the video sync signals.
Width of the horizontal sync pulse.
Width of the vertical sync pulse.
Number of active pixels per line (the horizontal resolution).
Number of active lines (the vertical resolution).
Number of clocks between the leading edge of the horizontal sync and the start of active data.
Number of lines between the leading edge of the vertical sync and the first line of active data.
M value for the video stream as computed by the source core in asynchronous clock mode.

detaileddescription.para: Must be written in synchronous mode.

briefdescription.para: Size of a transfer unit in the framing logic.
N value for the video stream as computed by the source core in asynchronous clock mode.

detaileddescription.para: Must be written in synchronous mode.

briefdescription.para: Selects the width of the user data input port.
Used to translate the number of pixels per line to the native internal 16-bit datapath.
The minimum number of bytes per transfer unit.
The fractional component when calculated the XDPPSU_MIN_BYTES_PER_TU register value.
Number of initial wait cycles at the start of a new line by the framing logic, allowing enough data to be buffered in the input FIFO.

sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: PHY configuration status.

briefdescription.para: Enable pseudo random bit sequence 7 pattern transmission for link quality assessment.
Instructs the PHY PLL to generate the proper clock frequency for the required link rate.

sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: DisplayPort audio.

briefdescription.para: Enables audio stream packets in main link and buffer control.
Used to input active channel count.
Word formatted as per CEA 861-C info frame.
M value of audio stream as computed by the DisplayPort TX core when audio and link clocks are synchronous.
N value of audio stream as computed by the DisplayPort TX core when audio and link clocks are synchronous.
Word formatted as per extension packet.

sectiondef.header: DPPSU core registers: Interrupts.
DPPSU core masks, shifts, and register values.

briefdescription.para: Training pattern 1 used for clock recovery.
Training pattern 2 used for channel equalization.
Training pattern 3 used for channel equalization for cores with DP v1.2.
Link quality test pattern not transmitted.
Symbol error rate measurement pattern transmitted.
Used for HBR2 compliance and 80-bit custom patterns.
Shift bits for core patch details.
Shift bits for core version revision.
Shift bits for core minor version.
Shift bits for core major version.
Shift bits for DisplayPort protocol revision.
Shift bits for DisplayPort protocol major version.
Shift bits for DisplayPort protocol major version.
Number of bytes to transfer with the current AUX command.
I2C-over-AUX write status MOT (middle-of- transaction) command.
Address only transfer enable (STOP will be sent after command).
Shift bits for AUX signal width filter.
Raw state of the HPD pin on the DP connector.
A request is currently being sent.
A reply is currently being received.
AUX transaction is complete and a valid reply transaction received.
AUX reply is currently being received.
AUX request is currently being transmitted.
Detected an error in the AUX reply of the most recent transaction.
Internal AUX reply state machine status bits.
Shift bits for the internal AUX reply state machine status.
Polarity of the horizontal sync pulse.
Polarity of the vertical sync pulse.
Shift bits for polarity of the vertical sync pulse.
Shift bits for stereo video attribute.
Received PHYSTATUS pulse from GT after rate change request from lane 0.
Received PHYSTATUS pulse from GT after rate change request from lane 1.
Detected an IRQ framed with the proper timing on the HPD signal.
Detected the presence of the HPD signal.
An AUX reply transaction has been detected.
A pulse on the HPD line was detected.
Extended packet has been transmitted and the core is ready to accept a new packet.
Interrupt asserted when live audio is enabled at subsystem, but the input does not match audio sample rate.
Interrupt at start of early vertical blanking.
When VCOUNT and HCOUNT from AV buffer manager register 0x078 matches early VCOUNT.
When VCOUNT and HCOUNT from AV buffer manager register 0x074 matches early VCOUNT.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 5 underflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 4 underflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 3 underflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 2 underflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 1 underflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 0 underflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 5 overflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 4 overflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 3 overflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 2 overflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 1 overflow.
AV buffer manager channel buffer 0 overflow.
Indicates that a user defined custom event 2 has triggered a timestamp.
Indicates that a user defined custom event has triggered a timestamp.
Indicates that an external VSYNC has triggered a timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: This is generated on every posedge of the external VSYNC signal.

briefdescription.para: Indicates that a VSYNC timestamp is available.

detaileddescription.para: This is generated on every VSYNC event.

briefdescription.para: Indicates that the soft reset has been set.
This field disables the DisplayPort core.
This field enables the DisplayPort core.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Receiver capability field.

description.para: Address mapping for the DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) of the downstream device.

sectiondef.header: DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link configuration field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link/sink status field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Automated testing sub-field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Sink control field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Sideband message buffers.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Event status indicator field.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Field addresses and sizes.
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Receiver capability field masks,
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link configuration field masks,
DisplayPort Configuration Data: Link/sink status field masks, shifts,
Extended Display Identification Data: Field addresses and sizes.

description.para: Address mapping for the Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) of the downstream device.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Register offsets for the

description.para: Tiled Display Topology (TDT) section data block.

sectiondef.header: Extended Display Identification Data: Masks, shifts, and register

description.para: values for the Tiled Display Topology (TDT) section data block.

sectiondef.header: Register access macro definitions.

briefdescription.para: This is a low-level function that reads from the specified register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the register offset to be read from.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the specified register.
C-style signature: u32 XDpPsu_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This is a low-level function that writes to the specified register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the register offset to write to.
is the 32-bit data to write to the specified register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XDpPsu_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: This header file contains the identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.

detaileddescription.para: High-level driver functions are defined in xdppsu.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 23/01/17 Initial release. 1.1 aad 10/04/17 Removed un-applicable registers
